claim: NVIDIA NemoClaw is an enterprise reference architecture wrapping OpenClaw with Nemotron models, OpenShell security runtime, and agent toolkits for safe deployment
actionable: Use NemoClaw for enterprise agent deployment; it provides security, local models, and policy controls out of the box

claim: Nemotron Super beats Llama 3.3 70B and GLM5 on PinchBench (OpenClaw compatibility), enabling local agent execution on NVIDIA GPUs
actionable: Use Nemotron Super for on-prem OpenClaw deployments if you need state-of-the-art agent performance with local control

claim: OpenShell provides YAML-based policy controls for agents (database access, network calls, cloud service permissions), preventing unauthorized actions automatically
actionable: Define OpenShell security policies to lock down agent access to sensitive systems; anything outside policy is auto-blocked

claim: Agent permissions should mirror employee permissions: scope agent capabilities as you would user roles in your organization
actionable: Design agent permission models matching your organizational structure; don't give all agents admin access
